label: Docker Sandbox
description: Run any CrewSwarm engine inside an isolated Docker microVM. Filesystem and network are sandboxed — API keys are injected by the network proxy and never exposed to the agent.
authNote: Requires Docker Desktop with Sandboxes enabled. Set CREWSWARM_DOCKER_SANDBOX_NAME to your sandbox name (default: crewswarm). Create once with: docker sandbox create --name crewswarm shell <workspace>
traits: Isolated microVM — agent cannot touch host filesystem outside workspace
Network proxy injects API keys — keys never exposed inside sandbox
Wraps any inner engine: Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor CLI, or Codex
setupSteps: Enable Docker Sandboxes in Docker Desktop → Settings → Sandboxes
Create a sandbox: docker sandbox create --name crewswarm shell <your-project-dir>
Allow localhost access: docker sandbox network proxy crewswarm --allow-host localhost
Set CREWSWARM_DOCKER_SANDBOX_NAME=crewswarm in dashboard → Security → Env Vars
